target.name: True Anomaly
target.founder: Evan Rogers, Kyle Zakrzewski, Daniel Brunski, Tom Nichols

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 17 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
True Anomaly, a Defense & Security software company at SPAC stage, is valued at $2.2B post-$1B+ raised. The peer universe comprises 17 same-sector comparables across similar valuation tiers, including 2 public names (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies. Top private peers include Anduril Industries, Saronic ($9.2B), Flock Safety ($7.5B), Shield AI ($5.6B), and Chaos Industries ($4.5B).

Peer-median valuation analysis applies directly to this cohort. The median across all 17 Defense & Security peers stands at $1.4B, with a range spanning $500M to $9.2B. This method anchors the primary valuation signal given the availability of a sufficiently large and relevant comp set operating at comparable maturity and sector focus.

The triangulated analysis yields a range of $1.2B to $1.6B, centered on the peer median of $1.4B. True Anomaly's current $2.2B valuation sits 57% above peer median, reflecting either a significant premium relative to the broader cohort or potential overvaluation relative to same-stage, same-sector benchmarks. Key uncertainties include the company's specific revenue profile, customer concentration, and technology differentiation versus the named peers, which the present data does not capture. The single-method triangulation also limits confidence; additional revenue or profitability-based approaches would strengthen the analysis.
